title: Listening - Part 4: Volunteering Abroad
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
topic: five people discussing their experiences volunteering in different countries

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’d always been interested in wildlife, but [21]I felt I needed to do something more practical than just donating money to charities[/21]. So, I spent three months at a turtle conservation project in Costa Rica. It was back-breaking work, patrolling beaches at night, but [26]seeing the direct impact of my efforts when the hatchlings reached the sea was incredibly moving[/26]. It made all the mosquito bites and lack of sleep worth it. You realize that one person actually can make a difference. || Speaker 2: After twenty years in accounting, I was looking for a way to use my professional skills in a completely different context. [22]I signed up to help a small NGO in Vietnam with their financial planning because I wanted to feel useful again[/22]. The cultural gap was wider than I expected, and [27]learning to adapt my communication style to a different cultural mindset was a huge learning curve[/27]. I thought I was there to teach them, but I ended up learning much more about patience and flexibility than I ever taught them about spreadsheets. || Speaker 3: I’d just finished university and felt completely lost about my future. [23]I decided to volunteer at a school in Ghana mainly to gain some perspective on my own life and problems[/23]. It was a humbling experience. What struck me most wasn't the poverty, but [28]the incredible warmth and hospitality shown to me by people who had so little[/28]. It completely changed my values. I went there thinking I was helping 'unfortunate' people, but I came back realizing that they were far richer in spirit and community than anyone I knew back home. || Speaker 4: I’m a keen gardener, and [24]I wanted to learn about traditional sustainable farming methods that aren't used in Europe anymore[/24]. I joined a community project in the Andes. The altitude was tough, but the knowledge was priceless. [29]The most rewarding part was the sense of accomplishment from building something sustainable that would benefit the village for years[/29]. We installed a new irrigation system using ancient techniques. It wasn't just about the plants; it was about preserving a way of life that is being lost to modernization. || Speaker 5: I’ve always been a bit of a homebody, and [25]my main motivation for volunteering at a marine center in Greece was to force myself to become more independent[/25]. I’d never traveled alone before. The work was demanding, and [30]finding the inner strength to cope with difficult situations without my usual support network was a real revelation[/30]. I discovered I’m much more resilient than I gave myself credit for. Now, I’m planning my next solo trip to Asia, something I would have been way too terrified to even consider a year ago.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21–25, choose from the list (A–H) what motivated each speaker to volunteer.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 wanted to do something more practical than just donating money.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 wanted to use his skills in a new context to feel useful again.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 wanted to gain perspective on her own life and problems.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 wanted to learn ancient sustainable farming techniques.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to force herself to become more independent.

options.D: to escape a difficult personal situation
options.E: to find a sense of purpose
options.G: to evaluate their own life choices

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26–30, choose from the list (A–H) what each speaker found most significant about their experience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 mentions seeing the direct impact of her efforts with the hatchlings.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 highlights the challenge and reward of adapting to a different cultural mindset.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 was moved by the warmth and hospitality of the local people.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 felt a sense of accomplishment from building something with long-term benefits.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 discovered her own resilience by coping without her usual support.

options.A: observing the tangible results of their work
options.D: the generosity of the local community
options.E: the beauty of the natural landscape
options.G: gaining a qualification in a new field
options.H: a newfound awareness of their own capabilities
